lorehq

Active Work Architecture
─[ K3s Operations ]─ ┐

K3s Operations

Purpose

Operate and troubleshoot the homelab K3s cluster.

Cluster Overview

  • Nodes: 3 (HA with embedded etcd)
  • CNI: Flannel (VXLAN)
  • LB: MetalLB [IP-Range]

Node Inventory

NodeHostRolesIPs
[node-1][host-1]control-plane,etcd[ips]
[node-2][host-2]control-plane,etcd[ips]
[node-3][host-3]control-plane,etcd[ips]

Access

  • Kubeconfig: Path to cluster config (e.g., ~/.kube/config).
  • SSH: ssh -i <identity_file> <user>@<node-ip>.

Storage Classes

NameTypeAccess ModeBackend
ceph-rbdBlock (RBD)RWOCeph RBD pool
nfsNFSRWXNFS Export
local-pathLocalRWONode filesystem (Default)

Common Operations

Health Check

kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl top nodes

Drain Node for Maintenance

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
# Do maintenance...
kubectl uncordon <node-name>

Rolling Upgrade

  1. Drain node.
  2. ssh to node and run: curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | INSTALL_K3S_CHANNEL=stable sh -
  3. Verify and uncordon.

Troubleshooting

Pod Stuck in Pending

  • Check kubectl describe pod <name>.
  • Check kubectl top nodes for resource constraints.
  • Verify PVC status: kubectl get pvc.

Storage Failures

  • RBD: Check kubectl logs -n ceph-csi-rbd -l app=ceph-csi-rbd.
  • NFS: Verify nfs-common is installed on nodes. Check kubectl logs -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs.

Secrets Management

NEVER put real secret values in manifests committed to git.

  1. Manifest uses PLACEHOLDER values.
  2. Use a patch-secrets.sh script to fetch from Vaultwarden (Bitwarden CLI) and apply via kubectl create secret ... --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -.
  3. Restart deployments: kubectl rollout restart deployment <name>.

Safety Rules

  • Always drain nodes before maintenance.
  • Upgrade one node at a time for HA stability.
  • Monitor etcd health before and after changes.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
